sources.title: Klavis Quadigy - Quad Envelope Generator (ModWiggler thread)

name: Quadigy
description: Quad 7-stage envelope generator (pre-delay, hold, attack, punch, decay, sustain, release) with 32 presets, a 156-point CV matrix, cycling with fit-to-clock, preset morphing, and Live/Studio modes. Doubles as multi-LFO and clock generator.

controls.name: Env 1-4 Buttons
controls.description: Select one or more envelopes for editing. Hold one and add others to edit several at once.
controls.detail: leftmost selected envelope drives the display; edits apply as absolute values to all selected

controls.name: Preset
controls.description: Opens the Preset menu (load, save, clear in Studio; load-only in Live).
controls.detail: 32 presets with naming; pairs with CV Matrix to clear CV assignments

controls.name: EG
controls.description: Opens the envelope generator menu: 3 fader pages (ADSR, extra timings, curves/level) plus 5 parameter pages.
controls.detail: in any fader page, pressing the encoder acts as a manual gate for selected envelopes

controls.name: Cycle
controls.description: Opens cycle and clock settings: source (off, individual, global, external), division/multiply, subdiv, offset, filling, gated cycle.
controls.detail: tap-tempo by tapping the encoder; new tempo set after 3 taps

controls.name: Global
controls.description: Opens the global menu: Quad Control Timing, preset control, morph mode, global morph time, brightness, screen saver, factory reset, firmware version.
controls.detail: long press toggles Studio / Live mode (Live LED lights and display shows S or L)

controls.name: CV Matrix
controls.description: Enables CV assignment for any CV-controllable parameter. LED flashes while active.
controls.detail: hold as shift and press the U/A/B menu buttons to pick the source, then move a fader / turn the encoder to set amount + polarity

controls.name: Live
controls.description: Indicator for Live mode. Hides risky editing pages and enables preset morphing / external preset control.
controls.detail: toggle via a long press of the Global button; morphing between presets only actually plays in Live mode

controls.name: Encoder
controls.description: Turn to select pages and change values; press to enter/confirm edits. In EG fader pages a press is a manual gate.
controls.detail: Edit LED lights blue while editing; 20 s inactivity timeout exits editing

controls.name: Faders 1-4
controls.description: On fader pages, adjust the four parameters shown. On the Global Quad Control Timing page each fader acts on its own envelope.
controls.detail: changes take effect after the fader crosses the current value (red LED goes solid); double pulses = zero or bipolar centre

controls.name: User knob
controls.description: Assignable bipolar virtual CV (labelled CV U on the matrix). Also used as U source selector when CV Matrix is active.
controls.detail: centre (zero) shown by double off-blips of its red LED, like a bipolar fader

inputs.name: G/T 1
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle-enable input for envelope 1. Mode depends on the envelope's Trigger Mode and Cycle settings.

inputs.name: G/T 2
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle-enable input for envelope 2.

inputs.name: G/T 3
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle-enable input for envelope 3.

inputs.name: G/T 4
inputs.description: Gate, trigger, or cycle-enable input for envelope 4.

inputs.name: CV A
inputs.description: External modulation source A. Doubles as preset-control input (trigger-step or 150 mV/preset CV) and as the audio input for firmware update.

inputs.name: CV B
inputs.description: External modulation source B for the CV matrix.

inputs.name: Clock
inputs.description: External master clock for cycling. Works with divide/multiply, subdiv and offset so cycling envelopes lock to an external tempo.

outputs.name: Env 1
outputs.description: Envelope output 1. Unipolar positive; Invert Output flips polarity but stays unipolar (0 to +max).

behaviors.name: Live vs Studio mode
behaviors.description: Studio mode exposes every setting; Live mode hides the fragile ones and enables preset morphing plus external preset control, so a stage patch can be tweaked without breaking it.

behaviors.name: Multi-editing
behaviors.trigger: hold one Env button, add others
behaviors.description: Several envelopes can be edited together. Changes are applied as absolute values to every selected channel; the leftmost one drives the display.

behaviors.name: Manual gate
behaviors.trigger: encoder press on any EG fader page
behaviors.description: On the ADSR, timings or curves fader pages, pressing the encoder fires a manual gate on the selected envelopes — fast auditioning without an external trigger.

behaviors.name: Cycling with fit-to-clock
behaviors.description: Each envelope can free-run, share the global internal clock, follow its own internal clock, or lock to the external Clock input. Phase times become percentages of one cycle so the overall shape stretches to tempo.

behaviors.name: Gated cycle
behaviors.trigger: Cycle menu, Gated cycle On, gate into G/T
behaviors.description: The G/T input becomes a run/stop gate. When the gate opens the envelope starts at its next offset point and cycles until the gate drops, then goes immediately to Release.

behaviors.name: Preset morphing
behaviors.description: Recalling a preset smoothly morphs all continuous settings and CV matrix amounts from the current preset to the new one, over 0-120 s. Morph time is read from the target preset (or from a global setting).

behaviors.name: Seven-stage envelopes
behaviors.trigger: gate or trigger into G/T, or cycle mode
behaviors.description: Stages are pre-delay, hold, attack, punch, decay, sustain, release. Hold virtually extends the gate; punch holds at max after attack; curves for A/D/R are continuous log-lin-exp; Full Attack, Join/Restart transition, Inverted Output and per-envelope level all tweak behaviour.

behaviors.name: 156-point CV matrix
behaviors.description: Three sources (CV A, CV B, User knob CV U) can each be routed with independent amount and polarity to 13 destinations per envelope, across all 4 envelopes — up to 156 simultaneous modulations. Output Level as a destination turns each envelope into a VCA.

behaviors.name: External preset control
behaviors.trigger: Global menu, Preset control set to Trigger or CV-A
behaviors.description: CV A can step through a user-defined range of presets by trigger, or select them by voltage (150 mV per preset from 0 V). Combines with morphing for hands-free scene changes.

behaviors.name: Global Quad Control Timing
behaviors.description: One fader per envelope, acting either as global time stretch, per-envelope tempo, global-clock tempo, or clock mult/divide depending on each section's cycle mode — a quick per-channel performance page.

patchIdeas.name: Four-envelope drum voice
patchIdeas.patch: Four gates into G/T 1-4 · Env 1-4 each driving its own VCA or filter · kick, snare, hat and accent envelopes from one 14 HP module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Mode: Studio (for setup) → Live (for the gig)
* Cycle: off on all 4
* EG: trigger mode per channel to taste

Sequencer:
Kick VCO:
Noise:
Kick VCA:
Snare VCA:
Hat VCA:
Accent bus:
Mixer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Trig 1) t> Quadigy (G/T 1)
- Sequencer (Trig 2) t> Quadigy (G/T 2)
- Sequencer (Trig 3) t> Quadigy (G/T 3)
- Sequencer (Trig 4) t> Quadigy (G/T 4)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> Kick VCA (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> Snare VCA (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 3) >> Hat VCA (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 4) >> Accent bus (CV)
- Kick VCO (Out) -> Kick VCA (In) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Noise (Out) -> Snare VCA (In) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Noise (Out) -> Hat VCA (In) -> Mixer (In 3)
- Mixer (Out) -> Output (In)
steps: Press [EG]. Select Env 1 with the top-row 1 button.
Set ADSR short on the first fader page: fast Attack, short Decay, zero Sustain, medium Release for a kick thump.
Select Env 2 and dial a classic snare envelope (slightly longer release).
Do the same for Env 3 (hat: very short) and Env 4 (accent: longer).
Patch four gates from your sequencer into G/T 1-4 and the four Env outputs into four VCAs / accent inputs.
Long-press [Global] so the Live LED lights — the patch is now protected from accidental editing on stage.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env 1-4: four independent 0 to +8 V percussive envelopes, one per drum voice.
patchIdeas.hear: A full four-element drum voice from one module — kick, snare, hat and accent all swing off their own envelopes with no cross-talk.

patchIdeas.name: Tempo-locked cycling pad
patchIdeas.patch: External clock into Clock · Env 1 and Env 2 cycling, fit-to-clock on, set to long slow shapes · they become two tempo-synced pseudo-LFOs that shape a filter and VCA of a chord.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Env 1: Cycle = External, Div /4, long A and R
* Env 2: Cycle = External, Div /2, Offset +3
* Mode: Live

Clock:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Quadigy (Clock)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)
steps: Patch an external clock into Clock.
Press [Cycle], select Env 1 and set Clock source to External, Divide /4, then give it a long Attack and Release on the EG page.
Select Env 2 and set Clock source to External, Divide /2, then add Offset +3 so it breathes against Env 1.
Patch Env 1 to your filter cutoff and Env 2 to a VCA on a held chord.
Long-press [Global] to go Live. Now any tempo change in the master clock re-stretches both envelopes automatically.
Play: speed up or slow the clock and both shapes follow without losing their ratio.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env 1 and Env 2: two 0 to +8 V cycling unipolar shapes whose lengths track the external clock.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow breathing pad that stays locked to the track's tempo — filter and amplitude swell in a shifting polyrhythm.

patchIdeas.name: Morphing preset scene
patchIdeas.patch: Preset 1 = snappy plucks. Preset 2 = long swells. In Live mode set a Morph Time of 8 s and either recall by hand or step with CV A — the whole envelope bank cross-fades between the two scenes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Preset 1: fast ADSR across Env 1-4
* Preset 2: slow ADSR + long Pre-delay across Env 1-4
* Global menu: Morph mode = Global, Global Morph Time = 8 s
* Mode: Live (morphing only runs in Live)

Sequencer:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCA A:
VCA B:
Filter:
Output:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> Quadigy (G/T 1)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Quadigy (G/T 2)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Quadigy (G/T 3)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> VCA A (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> VCA B (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 3) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO 1 (Out) -> VCA A (In)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> VCA B (In)
- VCA A (Out) -> Filter (In)
- VCA B (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)
steps: In Studio mode, tune Env 1-4 to a short, percussive ADSR. Press [Preset] and save as preset 1.
Edit the ADSR on all four envelopes to long swell-style shapes. Press [Preset] and save as preset 2.
Press [Global] and set Morph mode to Global, Global Morph Time to 8 s.
Long-press [Global] so the Live LED lights.
Press [Preset] and load preset 2 — watch the envelopes crossfade over 8 s while still firing from the same gates.
Optional: in Studio set Preset control to CV-A and patch a slow CV into CV A so presets step at 150 mV each.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env 1-4: same four outputs, but their shapes evolve over the morph time from snappy to slow.
patchIdeas.hear: Identical trigger pattern grows from tight plucks into long airy swells — the whole envelope bank transforms rather than switching abruptly.

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic multi-edit
patchIdeas.patch: Hold [1] and tap [2], [3], [4] so all four envelopes are selected at once · tweak a single fader and all four channels track the same change — ideal for a four-voice patch where every voice must behave consistently.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Env 1-4 selected together
* EG fader page: ADSR

Poly Keyboard:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:
VCA 1:
VCA 2:
VCA 3:
VCA 4:
Mixer:
Output:

- Poly Keyboard (Gate 1) g> Quadigy (G/T 1)
- Poly Keyboard (Gate 2) g> Quadigy (G/T 2)
- Poly Keyboard (Gate 3) g> Quadigy (G/T 3)
- Poly Keyboard (Gate 4) g> Quadigy (G/T 4)
- Quadigy (Env 1) >> VCA 1 (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 2) >> VCA 2 (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 3) >> VCA 3 (CV)
- Quadigy (Env 4) >> VCA 4 (CV)
- VCO 1 (Out) -> VCA 1 (In) -> Mixer (In 1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> VCA 2 (In) -> Mixer (In 2)
- VCO 3 (Out) -> VCA 3 (In) -> Mixer (In 3)
- VCO 4 (Out) -> VCA 4 (In) -> Mixer (In 4)
- Mixer (Out) -> Output (In)
steps: Patch four voice gates into G/T 1-4 and the four envelope outputs into four VCAs, one per voice.
Press [EG].
Hold top-row [1] and tap [2], [3], [4] — all four white LEDs stay lit. Multi-edit is active.
Move the Attack fader. All four envelopes take on the same absolute Attack time.
Do the same for Decay, Sustain and Release. The four voices now share one consistent envelope shape.
Release multi-edit by tapping a single Env button when you want to tune one voice differently.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env 1-4: four identical-shape envelopes, one per polyphonic voice.
patchIdeas.hear: A four-voice patch where the whole chord breathes together — every note has the exact same attack and release.

patchIdeas.name: LFO with waveshaping from an envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Env 3 in cycle mode with A and R set to linear at equal duration = triangle LFO · route the User knob via the CV matrix to A and R with opposite polarity · turning the knob morphs the triangle toward saw or ramp without changing the tempo.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Env 3: Cycle = Internal individual, curves linear
* CV Matrix: CV U → Env 3 Attack (+), CV U → Env 3 Release (-)
* Mode: Studio (so the matrix is editable)

VCO:
Filter:
Output:

- Quadigy (Env 3) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)
steps: Press [Cycle], select Env 3, set Clock source to Internal individual and a mid tempo.
Press [EG] and set Attack and Release to equal medium times, Sustain to zero, curves to linear (noon) — you now have a triangle.
Press [CV Matrix]. Hold [CV Matrix] and press the U button to pick the User knob as source.
On the EG curves/timings page, use the Attack fader to set a positive CV amount from U.
Repeat for Release but set a negative amount (opposite polarity).
Press [CV Matrix] again to exit. Patch Env 3 to a filter cutoff.
Twist the User knob: the triangle skews toward saw one way, ramp the other — the cycle length stays put.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env 3: a 0 to +8 V cycling waveform that morphs from saw through triangle to ramp under User knob control.
patchIdeas.hear: A smooth filter LFO whose waveform bends from up-saw through triangle to down-saw as you turn one knob, without the rate drifting.

patchIdeas.name: Clock generator and self-modulation
patchIdeas.patch: Env 4 cycled, A and R at zero, filling 50 percent = square clock out · route Env 1 into CV A and assign CV A → Env 4 tempo so Env 1's shape speeds up and slows down the clock — one module is now a modulated clock plus its own modulation source.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quadigy:
* Env 1: long cycling shape (Internal individual)
* Env 4: Cycle = Internal individual, A=0 R=0, Filling 50%, Invert off
* CV Matrix: CV A → Env 4 tempo

Drum voice:
Trig bus:

- Quadigy (Env 1) >> Quadigy (CV A)   // internal self-patch
- Quadigy (Env 4) c> Drum voice (Clock)
- Quadigy (Env 4) c> Trig bus (In)
steps: Press [Cycle], select Env 4, set Clock source to Internal individual, then on the EG page set Attack 0, Decay 0, Sustain max, Release 0.
In the Cycle menu set Filling to 50 percent — Env 4 now outputs a square clock.
Select Env 1, set Cycle = Internal individual and give it a long slow shape.
Patch Env 1 output into the CV A jack (internal self-patch).
Press [Cycle] again and with Env 4 selected, press [CV Matrix]. Hold [CV Matrix] + press the A button to pick CV A. On the tempo page, set a moderate positive CV amount.
Press [CV Matrix] to exit. Patch Env 4 to your drum voice's clock input.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env 4: 0 to +8 V square clock whose rate breathes with Env 1. Env 1: long slow control voltage used internally.
patchIdeas.hear: A tempo-modulated clock that accelerates and decelerates on its own, great for rubato drum patterns driven by one module.
